title: Jenkins の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately describes common causes and solutions for Jenkins 403 errors.
The explanations provided are consistent with official Jenkins documentation and community knowledge.
Practical code examples are included, which enhances the article's utility.
The recommendations regarding security (e.g., keeping CSRF enabled, using API tokens) align with best practices.

sources.title: Matrix-based security - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins's 403 Forbidden error means that the user failed an authorization check and occurs due to rejection by CSRF protection or because the user lacks necessary permissions.

sources.title: Troubleshoot the HTTP Error 403 (Forbidden) for Jenkins - Site24x7 Support
sources.claim: Jenkins's 403 Forbidden error means that the user failed an authorization check and occurs due to rejection by CSRF protection or because the user lacks necessary permissions.

sources.title: Getting 403, when try to Build a Job - Ask a question - Jenkins community
sources.claim: Jenkins's 403 Forbidden error means that the user failed an authorization check.

sources.title: CSRF Protection - Jenkins
sources.claim: The error frequently occurs when automatically executing jobs in CI/CD pipelines or calling the Jenkins API from external tools.

sources.title: Jenkins HTTP Status 403-Forbidden Error - Ask a question
sources.claim: The actual error message example includes: "error": "403 Forbidden", "message": "No valid crumb was included in the request".

sources.title: CSRF Protection - Jenkins
sources.claim: If Jenkins' CSRF protection is enabled, a Crumb token is required for POST requests (such as executing jobs, changing settings).

sources.title: Jenkins CSRF CRUMB - KodeKloud Docs
sources.claim: If Jenkins' CSRF protection is enabled, a Crumb token is required for POST requests (such as executing jobs, changing settings).

sources.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) Protection - BMC Documentation
sources.claim: If Jenkins' CSRF protection is enabled, a Crumb token is required for POST requests (such as executing jobs, changing settings) and if a request is made without this token, a 403 error is returned.

sources.title: Learn How To Call The Jenkins REST API From PowerShell | Octopus blog
sources.claim: If crumb acquisition fails, check if Jenkins has CSRF protection enabled and verify that the API token is correct.

sources.title: Jenkins Matrix-based security authorization - Mirantis Documentation Portal
sources.claim: If Jenkins' permission settings use Matrix-based security or Role-based Access Control, a 403 error occurs if the user does not have permission to build or configure jobs.

sources.title: How to Configure RBAC in Jenkins | Baeldung on Ops
sources.claim: If Jenkins' permission settings use Matrix-based security or Role-based Access Control, a 403 error occurs if the user does not have permission to build or configure jobs and permission granting is done in the Jenkins Web UI via 'Manage Jenkins → Configure Global Security → Authorization' section.

sources.title: Security Pipeline Matrix - KodeKloud
sources.claim: If the intersection of the user row and job permission column is not checked in the permission matrix, it will be rejected.

sources.title: Jenkins Security Tips | Octopus blog
sources.claim: If the intersection of the user row and job permission column is not checked in the permission matrix, it will be rejected and permission granting is done in the Jenkins Web UI via 'Manage Jenkins → Configure Global Security → Authorization' section.

sources.title: Authenticating scripted clients - Jenkins
sources.claim: If Jenkins prohibits anonymous access, a 403 error is returned if the API is called without authentication information and this error is likely to occur when accessing Jenkins from external CI/CD tools or monitoring scripts, if API token generation or specification is forgotten.

sources.title: API Token in Jenkins REST API - CloudBees
sources.claim: How to get an API token: Jenkins management screen → User List → Click target user → Configure → API Token section, generate a new token.

sources.title: Jenkins User Management API Guide - Stitchflow
sources.claim: How to get an API token: Jenkins management screen → User List → Click target user → Configure → API Token section, generate a new token.

sources.title: Connect to Jenkins using API token authentication - ServiceNow
sources.claim: How to get an API token: Jenkins management screen → User List → Click target user → Configure → API Token section, generate a new token.

sources.title: Securing Jenkins: Configuring CSRF Protection for Enhanced Web Application Security | by Ramkrushna Maheshwar | Medium
sources.claim: Open 'Manage Jenkins → Configure Global Security' in the Jenkins management screen, and check the 'CSRF Protection' section. If 'Prevent Cross Site Request Forgery exploits' is checked, CSRF protection is enabled.

sources.title: CSRF Protection Explained - CloudBees Documentation
sources.claim: Open 'Manage Jenkins → Configure Global Security' in the Jenkins management screen, and check the 'CSRF Protection' section. If 'Prevent Cross Site Request Forgery exploits' is checked, CSRF protection is enabled.

sources.title: Jenkins Plugin · digital.ai Release
sources.claim: Open 'Manage Jenkins → Configure Global Security' in the Jenkins management screen, and check the 'CSRF Protection' section. If 'Prevent Cross Site Request Forgery exploits' is checked, CSRF protection is enabled.

sources.title: CSRF Protection - Jenkins
sources.claim: Conversely, if you want to disable it (though not recommended for security), uncheck it here.

sources.title: Remote API - Jenkins
sources.claim: In Jenkins, a token generated via the /user/<username>/generateApiToken endpoint is used, and authentication is done in username:api-token format with HTTP Basic Authentication.

sources.title: Role-based Authorization Strategy - Jenkins Plugins
sources.claim: If the Role-based Access Control (RBAC) plugin is installed, fine-grained permissions such as "Job/Build" and "Job/Configure" can be set in the role definition.

sources.title: CloudBees Role-Based Access Control Plugin
sources.claim: If the Role-based Access Control (RBAC) plugin is installed, fine-grained permissions such as "Job/Build" and "Job/Configure" can be set in the role definition.

sources.title: Implementing Identity and Access Management (IAM) on Jenkins Using the Role-Based Strategy Plugin - DEV Community
sources.claim: If the Role-based Access Control (RBAC) plugin is installed, fine-grained permissions such as "Job/Build" and "Job/Configure" can be set in the role definition, and managing permissions by role instead of individual users makes operations easier.

sources.title: How do you implement role-based access control RBAC in Jenkins to secure pipelines
sources.claim: If the Role-based Access Control (RBAC) plugin is installed, fine-grained permissions such as "Job/Build" and "Job/Configure" can be set in the role definition.

sources.title: Viewing logs - Jenkins
sources.claim: Check <Jenkins Home>/logs/jenkins.log or Jenkins management screen's System Log. "Permission denied" or "Insufficient permission" messages should be recorded.

sources.title: Remote Access API - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins official Remote API documentation describes the detailed specifications for API calls.

sources.title: Script Console - Jenkins
sources.claim: You can directly execute and test Groovy scripts in 'Manage Jenkins → Script Console'.

sources.title: Manage Jenkins Using Script Console | by Nilkumar Shah | Globant | Medium
sources.claim: You can directly execute and test Groovy scripts in 'Manage Jenkins → Script Console'.

sources.title: Jenkins Script Console — a magnificent way to perform automation | by Daniel Laettner
sources.claim: You can directly execute and test Groovy scripts in 'Manage Jenkins → Script Console'.

sources.title: Jenkins : Script console option - Medium
sources.claim: You can directly execute and test Groovy scripts in 'Manage Jenkins → Script Console'.

sources.title: Permissions - Jenkins
sources.claim: When calling the Jenkins API within a Groovy script, the permissions of the script's executing user apply.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
